messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: It is mine, will do the final deployment this weekend. First set it up as a room server, but in that location it makes more sense as a repeater. Still a bit MeshCore-lonely here in Switzerland Where are you located?
messages.content_html: It is mine, will do the final deployment this weekend. First set it up as a room server, but in that location it makes more sense as a repeater. Still a bit MeshCore-lonely here in Switzerland Where are you located?

messages.text: Actualy it’s a room server

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: I know, that is how it is currently configured. Still a bit unsure, but currently have a tendency to just make this a repeater and then run a seperate room server in my place. Will get updated and an RTC module added this weekend.
messages.content_html: I know, that is how it is currently configured. Still a bit unsure, but currently have a tendency to just make this a repeater and then run a seperate room server in my place. Will get updated and an RTC module added this weekend.

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: You're in Switzerland too? I just sourced a couple of these solar cases, have some spare if you happen to be local. I am mainly running the RAK devices. For solar and mobile they are simply the best cause of the low power usage. I also don't need a display on any of my nodes.
messages.content_html: You're in Switzerland too? I just sourced a couple of these solar cases, have some spare if you happen to be local. I am mainly running the RAK devices. For solar and mobile they are simply the best cause of the low power usage. I also don't need a display on any of my nodes.

messages.author: 6Toes_PlanetApe
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: That does look very similar. I live in a foggz area, that is why I went for the bigger panel.
messages.content_html: That does look very similar. I live in a foggz area, that is why I went for the bigger panel.

messages.author: 6Toes_PlanetApe
messages.text: Ok, i discovered my solar panel is not as efficient as i would like. Yours have mppt and that’s more efficient.
messages.content_html: Ok, i discovered my solar panel is not as efficient as i would like. Yours have mppt and that’s more efficient.

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: The one on the image does not have MPPT. I am currently comparing it to one with MPPT to figure out if it really makes a differences.
messages.content_html: The one on the image does not have MPPT. I am currently comparing it to one with MPPT to figure out if it really makes a differences.

messages.reply_html:
chixxxi
Are they running SF10 or SF11? Doesn't say it for the repeaters on the map, but I saw some client nodes using SF11.

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: SF11 will reach a bit further, but it is slower and therefore provides less bandwidth.
messages.content_html: SF11 will reach a bit further, but it is slower and therefore provides less bandwidth.

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: IMPORTANT: This morning we changed the nodes in the Bern / Biel are from SF=10 to SF=11. We are planning to test this for a couple days to check if this is even better.
messages.content_html: IMPORTANT: This morning we changed the nodes in the Bern / Biel are from SF=10 to SF=11. We are planning to test this for a couple days to check if this is even better.

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: 100km auch nicht, aber 60-70 haben wir schon. Mal schauen was die Tests Morgen ergeben Hasenmatt (Berg bei Solothurn im Hura) zu Hornbergbecken in Deutschland wird Morgen wohl einer der interessantesten, das sind 60km. Mit den aktuellen Einstellungen ist die verbindung zwar da, aber instabil, bin sehr gespannt drauf was Narrow da für einen Einfluss hat.
messages.content_html: 100km auch nicht, aber 60-70 haben wir schon. Mal schauen was die Tests Morgen ergeben Hasenmatt (Berg bei Solothurn im Hura) zu Hornbergbecken in Deutschland wird Morgen wohl einer der interessantesten, das sind 60km. Mit den aktuellen Einstellungen ist die verbindung zwar da, aber instabil, bin sehr gespannt drauf was Narrow da für einen Einfluss hat.

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: Genau, die Test waren so gut, es gab sogar Beschwerden aus Deutschland https://freiburg.social/@blackforestmesh/115315520831442911
messages.content_html: Genau, die Test waren so gut, es gab sogar Beschwerden aus Deutschland https://freiburg.social/@blackforestmesh/115315520831442911
embeds_html:
BLACK FOREST MESH (@blackforestmesh@freiburg.social)
Liebe MeshCore Nutzende, vor allem liebe AFUs in der Schweiz, Ich freue mich, dass MeshCore so gut funktioniert. Und ich freue mich für euch Schweizer, dass ihr so tolle hohe Standorte habt und mit toller Leistung senden dürft. Aber bitte bitte richtet euch für eure Plaudereien doch einen eigenen Kanal ein, statt auf Public alle im Umkreis von 300 km an euren Unterhaltungen teilhaben zu lassen. Die Akzeptanz meiner Frau, den Companion bei sich zu tragen, sinkt rapide, seit sie ständig wegen irrelevanter Nachrichten vom Companion genervt wird. Lasst uns Public bitte als Anrufkanal sehen, ähnlich wie im Seefunk und im CB-Funk. Danke! #meshcore #schweiz #swiss #switzerland #bern #afu #hamradio #ham #funkdisziplin
freiburg.social • 2025-10-04 6:53 a.m.

messages.text: Nice to see the french part of Switzerland join too! Put up a few repeaters and people will join. messages.author: Olli
messages.text: Ich wohne in Freiburg im Breisgau, bin aber Schweizer:-) und empfange sehr viel aus der Schweiz (In FR). Ob Du Bewilligungen bekommst...(?) Aber Du kannst ja bei einem Bergbauern fragen der eine Alphütte hat die gut gelegen ist... Gemmi wäre da so ein Standort um ins Berner Oberland zu kommen! Da kannst Du ja an der Seilbahn mal fragen...

messages.author: Bullit
messages.text: Naja das mit den Bergbauern ist auch so eine Sache. An die muss man erstmal rankommen. Wohne ja im Tal und nicht in den Berg-Regionen. Muss mal langfristig schauen ob es eine gute Verbindungsmöglichkeit zum restlichen Mesh gibt. Zuerst mal das Oberwallis allgemein erschliessen. Hier ist nichts - bin der Einzige Fleck auf der Map…. Und wegen den Bewilligungen bzw. Erlaubnis von Gemeinden etc. - Notfalls halt Guerilla-Style und hoffen, dass die Dinger dranbleiben xD
messages.content_html: Naja das mit den Bergbauern ist auch so eine Sache. An die muss man erstmal rankommen. Wohne ja im Tal und nicht in den Berg-Regionen. messages.author: Herobrainiac
messages.text: Hi peeps! It was just the other day that I learned about MeshCore and I am now absolutely baffled by the progress that Switzerland has had in the last year! I fear that my City of Uster (south of Zürich) lacks signal to the wider Network so I was thinking of supporting the Mesh with my own repeater in the region :) In order to do so on the cheap, while not compromising on a good repeater, as I started building out the setup, I landed on the RAK19007 for its low power draw, (hopefully) making it capable of surviving the next winter on solar. As for the box; I found an official Unify one on AliExpress with all the nice to haves. But as it is 42CHF, I was hesitant to buy it from there because of the risk of... well... AliExpress. The box in question, Model Nr. 910406 Turns out I seem unable to find the same box with all the nice to haves (important, since if not it's quite a bit more expensive than just a comparable generic IP65/67 box). So my question to you guys is whether AliExpress is really that risky to buy from, how your expiriences have been and possible alternatives I might have missed? Thanks in advance ^^ (edited)
messages.content_html: Hi peeps! It was just the other day that I learned about MeshCore and I am now absolutely baffled by the progress that Switzerland has had in the last year! I fear that my City of Uster (south of Zürich) lacks signal to the wider Network so I was thinking of supporting the Mesh with my own repeater in the region :) In order to do so on the cheap, while not compromising on a good repeater, as I started building out the setup, I landed on the RAK19007 for its low power draw, (hopefully) making it capable of surviving the next winter on solar. As for the box; I found an official Unify one on AliExpress with all the nice to haves. But as it is 42CHF, I was hesitant to buy it from there because of the risk of... well... AliExpress. The box in question, Model Nr. 910406 Turns out I seem unable to find the same box with all the nice to haves (important, since if not it's quite a bit more expensive than just a comparable generic IP65/67 box). So my question to you guys is whether AliExpress is really that risky to buy from, how your expiriences have been and possible alternatives I might have missed? Thanks in advance ^^ (edited)

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: Make sure to check out our very active Community on https://www.meshcore.ch/ and https://community.meshcore.ch/ too! @Herobrainiac We are working on the next Sammelbestellung from China for the D5 Repeater. We usually end up at about CHF35 everything included but the actual RAK Board. Check it out here and add your wish quickly if you want to take part: https://community.meshcore.ch/t/sammelbestellung-d5-solar-repeater/80/57
messages.content_html: Make sure to check out our very active Community on https://www.meshcore.ch/ and https://community.meshcore.ch/ too! @Herobrainiac We are working on the next Sammelbestellung from China for the D5 Repeater. We usually end up at about CHF35 everything included but the actual RAK Board. Check it out here and add your wish quickly if you want to take part: https://community.meshcore.ch/t/sammelbestellung-d5-solar-repeater/80/57
embeds_html:
MeshCore Switzerland Community
MeshCore Switzerland is where you find all important links for getting started with MeshCore and meet other Swiss and worldwide MeshCore users.

MeshCore Switzerland
Off-line and Off-grid messaging platform for a new era of secure communications.

Sammelbestellung - D5 Solar Repeater
Harte Verhandlungen mit China, die wollten höhere Preise wegen dem RAM Kosten geltend machen, den lasse ich in einen Produkt ohne RAM aber nicht durch gehen Hoffe ich kann bis Ende Woche eine erste Prognose für die Ankunft der Bestellung durch geben.

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: Bastelgarage has a few of the Repeater cases you are looking at if you prefer buying in Switzerland: https://www.bastelgarage.ch/index.php?route=product/search&search=wismesh
messages.content_html: Bastelgarage has a few of the Repeater cases you are looking at if you prefer buying in Switzerland: https://www.bastelgarage.ch/index.php?route=product/search&search=wismesh

Dear community. The TRANSALP project is live. Connecting Ticino (and more) to the north. Have fun! Werte Community. Das Transalp Projekt lebt. Wir verbinden den Norden mit dem Tessin (und mehr). Geniesst es! Kind regards / beste Grüsse Christoph / HB9DTZ

embeds_html:
Solar Mesh Node | MeshNodes
nRF52840 based Solar Mesh Node is a Low Power Consumption development board based on nRF52840 and SX1262 and supports LoRa and Bluetooth 5.0. This nodes includes a battery and a solar panel for remote applications.

messages.author: Bullit
messages.text: Nicht böse nehmen! Das Design bzw der Formfaktor/Grösse gefällt mir super. Aber für 1 Franken mehr, kann ich mir einen Seeed SenseCap P1 Pro mit 5 Watt Solarzelle, 4x 18650 Zellen, GPS, Xiao usw holen. Inkl. diverser Mounting-Optionen. Finde deinen Node echt hübsch und schön klein. Aber preislich, zumindest für mich, unattraktiv. Auch wenn gute Arbeit nun mal etwas kostet.
messages.content_html: Nicht böse nehmen! Das Design bzw der Formfaktor/Grösse gefällt mir super. messages.author: Nemo 🇨🇭
messages.text: Hey, mal eine Frage in die Runde, an MeshCore-User mit Kontakt zum "grossen gemeinsamen Mesh" Gibt es da auch richtigen Austausch untereinander, technische Unterstützung und so?
messages.content_html: Hey, mal eine Frage in die Runde, an MeshCore-User mit Kontakt zum "grossen gemeinsamen Mesh" Gibt es da auch richtigen Austausch untereinander, technische Unterstützung und so?

messages.author: chixxxi
messages.text: Da gibt es schon richtigen Austausch, hängt vom Channel ab. #test und #chtest ist hauptsächlich Ping-Poing. In #Switzerland ist das aber nicht erwünscht, da plaudern wir richtig unter Menschen.
